error.signature: AI tells someone in Malaysia to call 911 in an emergency

verdict.summary: Malaysia does not use the US 911. Dial 999 for police, ambulance, and fire; 112 also works from mobile phones and routes to 999. Both are free from any phone.

dead_ends.action: Dial 911 in Malaysia expecting to reach emergency services
dead_ends.why_fails: 911 is not the emergency number in Malaysia. The emergency number is 999 for all services. A 911 call does not connect to local police, fire, or ambulance.
dead_ends.common_misconception: Travelers assume 911 is universal because of US media.

dead_ends.action: Dial 911 expecting it to work in Malaysia
dead_ends.why_fails: Malaysia's emergency number is 999, not 911. While 112 from a mobile routes to 999, 911 is not the assigned number.

workarounds.action: From a mobile you can dial 112, which connects to the 999 emergency response centre (MERS 999).
workarounds.how: 999 from any phone, or 112 from a mobile. State the service you need and your location.
workarounds.tradeoff: 112 is a mobile fallback; 999 is primary.
